The section component \((f_i).\mathtt{app}(P)\) is, by construction, linear over the restriction-of-scalars action carried by \((M|_{\iota _i})(P)\) — restriction of scalars along the structure-ring map of the open immersion \(\iota _i\) — whereas the \(f\)-leg of the goal feeds in the native \(\mathcal{O}_X(\mathrm{image})\)-module action. These two actions are propositionally equal but not definitionally equal. The reconciliation is the genuine content of (c): for an open immersion the structure-ring comparison isomorphism is the identity — \(\mathtt{appIso}(\iota _i) = \mathtt{Iso.refl}\) — so the ring map along which scalars are restricted is the identity \(\mathbf{1}\); and restriction of scalars along the identity ring map returns the original action, \(c \cdot _{\, \mathrm{restr}\, \mathbf{1}} x = \mathbf{1}(c) \cdot x = c \cdot x\), identified by \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul\_ def}\) together with \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsId'App}\). This identity-ring-map smul bridge — the localization observation that restriction of scalars along an isomorphism that happens to be the identity reproduces the native module action — is the substantive mathematics of sub-step (c).

  • (c.iii) The \(N\)-leg (target semilinearity). Once the bridge of (c.ii) is in place, the target leg is discharged by the native identity \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.map\_ smul}\, N\), exactly as in (c.i); the scalars transported across the two legs then reconcile because the two open-set \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) maps arising from the down-set identity \(\iota _i\bigl(\iota _i^{-1}(W)\bigr) = W\) compose to the identity on the overlap.

(ii) Promote to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear. The linearity equation \(g(r \cdot m) = r \cdot g(m)\) is a sectionwise identity between two sections of a separated presheaf. It holds on each \(U_i\), because there \(g\) restricts to the module morphism \(f_i\); since the two sides agree on the cover \(\{ U_i\} \) and the ambient presheaf is separated, they agree globally. Packaging \(g\) together with this global linearity through the scheme-level wrapper 517 (\(\mathtt{homMk}\)) yields a morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in \(\mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\); its restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\) and its uniqueness are both inherited from the ab-sheaf amalgamation of step (i).

This gluing engine is substantially larger than a routine glue: it is dominated by the \(\mathtt{localSection}\) naturality condition and the convert-and-recover bookkeeping, and the naturality is the section-direction slice of 500 — so its true cost is bounded below by building that sheaf-site equivalence, not by the surrounding assembly. It is therefore to be scoped as a multi-piece build — with \(\mathtt{localSection}\), together with its naturality, built as a standalone verified lemma first — rather than a one-shot. The step computes no tensor stalk — it is purely a statement about gluing morphisms of sheaves — and so it does not re-enter the stalk-tensor commutation.

Remark 523 How the dual infrastructure discharges the \(\otimes \)-inverse
#

Uses

  • Lemma 421
  • Lemma 515
  • Lemma 522
  • Lemma 516
  • Lemma 407
  • Lemma 419

Once ?? are available, 421 is discharged with no further missing object: take \(L^{-1} := \mathtt{dual}\, L\) (495), which is a line bundle by 515. The global contraction \(\varepsilon _L \colon L \otimes _X L^{-1} \to \mathcal{O}_X\) is not obtained by sheafifying the presheaf-level evaluation; it is instead built by gluing the canonical local contraction morphisms. On each trivialising open \(U\), the restriction-compatibility iso 407 carries \((L \otimes _X L^{-1})|_U\) to \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes _U \mathcal{O}_U\), and the canonical local contraction \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes _U \mathcal{O}_U \to \mathcal{O}_U\) of the free rank-one module with its dual is the left unitor 419, an isomorphism. These local contractions are canonical, hence agree on overlaps, and so glue — by the A-bridge 522 — to a single global morphism \(\varepsilon _L \colon L \otimes _X L^{-1} \to \mathcal{O}_X\). Since “being an isomorphism” is local on \(X\) and \(\varepsilon _L\) restricts to an isomorphism on each \(U\), it is a global isomorphism \(L \otimes _X L^{-1} \xrightarrow {\sim } \mathcal{O}_X\) by the B-connector 516.

This descent construction deliberately avoids the forbidden “sheafify-the-evaluation” route — which would re-enter the abandoned tensor-stalk / sheafification-unit whiskering commutation — and computes no tensor stalk anywhere. It is the realization the “infrastructure-blocked” note in 421 points to.

Remark 524 Alternative route via object-level descent (fallback)
#

Should the slice-and-sheafify route of ?? bottom out, the dual can instead be assembled by object-level descent: glue the local trivial duals \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) along the inverse-transpose transition isomorphisms \(g_{ij}^{-\mathsf{T}}\) of \(L\) into a global \(L^{-1}\) on \(X\). Abstractly this is effective descent for sheaves of modules on the small Zariski site — the essential-surjectivity of the descent-data functor of an \(\mathtt{IsStack}\) pseudofunctor \(U \mapsto (\mathcal{O}_U\text{-modules})\). This is the more principled and reusable construction but the larger build (it requires constructing the restriction pseudofunctor and proving the stack/effective-descent property), and is recorded only as a fallback; the slice internal-hom route above is the intended one. It is the same morphism-/object-level descent family on which the associator re-route of 418 also waits.

20.13.2 Internal-hom and restrict-scalars helper declarations

The following are the internal Lean helper declarations assembling the morphism module 489, the slice internal hom 491, its restriction maps 492, the presheaf dual 493, the evaluation morphism 494, the lax-monoidal restrict-scalars structure 406, the ring-iso base-change equivalence 409 and the pushforward adjunction 408. Each is proved directly in Lean and carries the dependency edges of the construction.

Definition 525 Canonical ring map from a terminal object
#

Uses

  • Definition 489
  • Definition 489

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.termRingMap

For a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) on a base category with terminal object \(T\) and an object \(Y\), the unique morphism \(Y \to T\) induces the ring map \(R(T) \to R(Y)\) along which a global scalar acts on the value at \(Y\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, as \(R\) applied to the opposite of the terminal morphism.

Lemma 526 Naturality of the terminal ring map
#

Uses

  • Definition 525
  • Definition 525

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.termRingMap_naturality

The restriction map \(R(g) : R(X) \to R(Y)\) along \(g : X \to Y\) carries \(\mathtt{termRingMap}_X\, f\) to \(\mathtt{termRingMap}_Y\, f\), so the images of a global scalar \(f \in R(T)\) are compatible with restriction.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, from uniqueness of morphisms into the terminal object and functoriality of \(R\).

Lemma 527 The terminal ring map at the terminal object is the identity
#

Uses

  • Definition 525
  • Definition 525

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.termRingMap_terminal

At the terminal object itself the induced ring map \(R(T) \to R(T)\) is the identity, since the unique \(T \to T\) is \(\mathrm{id}_T\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, using \(\mathtt{IsTerminal.hom\_ ext}\) and \(R.\mathtt{map\_ id}\).

Definition 528 Multiplication by a global scalar as an endomorphism
#

Uses

  • Definition 525
  • Lemma 526
  • Definition 525
  • Lemma 526

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.globalSMul

For \(f \in R(T)\), the endomorphism \(m_f^{N} : N \to N\) of a presheaf of modules \(N\) that at each object \(Y\) is multiplication by \(\mathtt{termRingMap}_Y\, f \in R(Y)\); its naturality is the heart of the global-scalar action on morphism modules.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: the naturality square commutes by \(\mathtt{termRingMap\_ naturality}\) and the semilinearity of the restriction maps of \(N\).

Lemma 529 Sectionwise action of the global-scalar endomorphism
#

Uses

  • Definition 528
  • Definition 528

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.globalSMul_hom_apply

At an object \(Y\), \(m_f^{N}\) acts on a section as multiplication by \(\mathtt{termRingMap}_Y\, f\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, by definitional unfolding.

Lemma 530 The global-scalar endomorphism at one is the identity
#

Uses

  • Definition 528
  • Definition 528

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.globalSMul_one

\(m_1^{N} = \mathrm{id}_N\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, from \(\mathtt{map\_ one}\) and \(1 \cdot s = s\).

Lemma 531 The global-scalar endomorphism at zero is zero
#

Uses

  • Definition 528
  • Definition 528

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.globalSMul_zero

\(m_0^{N} = 0\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, from \(\mathtt{map\_ zero}\) and \(0 \cdot s = 0\).

Lemma 532 Additivity of the global-scalar endomorphism
#

Uses

  • Definition 528
  • Definition 528

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.globalSMul_add

\(m_{f+g}^{N} = m_f^{N} + m_g^{N}\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, from \(\mathtt{map\_ add}\) and \((f+g) \cdot s = f \cdot s + g \cdot s\).

Lemma 533 Multiplicativity of the global-scalar endomorphism
#

Uses

  • Definition 528
  • Definition 528

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.globalSMul_mul

\(m_{fg}^{N} = m_g^{N} \circ m_f^{N}\): ring multiplication becomes composition, the order reversed because the action is by post-composition.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, from \(\mathtt{map\_ mul}\) and iterated scalar multiplication.

Definition 534 Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category
#

Uses

  • Definition 489
  • Definition 489

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.restr

The restriction \(M|_U\) of the slice internal-hom formula: the pushforward of \(M\) along the over-category projection \(\mathtt{Over.forget}\, U\), whose base ring at the terminal \(\mathtt{Over.mk}\, (\mathrm{id}_U)\) is \(R(U)\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, as \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pushforward}_0\) along \(\mathtt{Over.forget}\, U\).

Lemma 535 Component agreement up to heterogeneous equality
#

Uses

  • Lemma 492
  • Lemma 492

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.hom_app_heq

The component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two equal objects agrees up to heterogeneous equality.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by substituting the object equality.

Lemma 536 Identity functoriality of the restriction map
#

Uses

  • Lemma 492
  • Lemma 492

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.restrictionMap_id

Restricting along the identity \(U \to U\) is the identity on morphism modules.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, using \(\mathtt{Over.mapId}\) and the heterogeneous component agreement 535.

Lemma 537 Composition functoriality of the restriction map
#

Uses

  • Lemma 492
  • Lemma 492

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.restrictionMap_comp

Restricting along \(h \circ g\) is restricting along \(g\) then along \(h\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, using \(\mathtt{Over.mapComp}\) and 535.

Lemma 538 The restriction map respects composition of morphisms
#

Uses

  • Lemma 492
  • Lemma 492

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.restrictionMap_comp_hom

Restriction carries a composite \(\varphi \circ \psi \) of morphisms of restricted modules to the composite of the restrictions.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, by functoriality of the pushforward.

Lemma 539 Additivity of the restriction map
#

Uses

  • Lemma 492
  • Lemma 492

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.restrictionMap_add

The restriction map is additive in the morphism it restricts.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, sectionwise.

Lemma 540 The restriction map preserves zero
#

Uses

  • Lemma 492
  • Lemma 492

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.restrictionMap_zero

The restriction map sends the zero morphism to the zero morphism.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, sectionwise.

Lemma 541 Restriction commutes with the global-scalar endomorphism
#

Uses

  • Lemma 492
  • Definition 528
  • Lemma 526
  • Lemma 492
  • Definition 528
  • Lemma 526

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.restrictionMap_globalSMul

Restricting the multiplication-by-\(r\) endomorphism of \(N|_U\) along \(g : V \to U\) yields the multiplication-by-\(R(g)(r)\) endomorphism of \(N|_V\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: both sides act sectionwise by scalar multiplication and the scalars agree by \(\mathtt{termRingMap\_ naturality}\).

Lemma 542 Semilinearity of the restriction map
#

Uses

  • Lemma 492
  • Lemma 538
  • Lemma 541
  • Lemma 492
  • Lemma 538
  • Lemma 541

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.restrictionMap_smul

For \(f : X \to Y\), a global scalar \(r \in R(X)\) and \(m\), \(\mathtt{restrictionMap}\, (r \cdot m) = R(f)(r) \cdot \mathtt{restrictionMap}\, m\) — the compatibility datum consumed by the presheaf assembly.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, from \(\mathtt{restrictionMap\_ comp\_ hom}\) and \(\mathtt{restrictionMap\_ globalSMul}\).

Definition 543 Restriction map as an additive group homomorphism
#

Uses

  • Lemma 492
  • Lemma 539
  • Lemma 540
  • Lemma 492
  • Lemma 539
  • Lemma 540

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.restrictionMapAddHom

The restriction map packaged as an additive group homomorphism on the morphism groups, using its additivity and zero-preservation.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, bundling \(\mathtt{restrictionMap\_ zero}\) and \(\mathtt{restrictionMap\_ add}\).

Definition 544 Underlying abelian-group presheaf of the internal hom
#

Uses

  • Lemma 492
  • Definition 543
  • Lemma 536
  • Lemma 537
  • Lemma 492
  • Definition 543
  • Lemma 536
  • Lemma 537

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.internalHomPresheaf

The \(\mathbf{Ab}\)-valued presheaf \(U \mapsto (M|_U \to N|_U)\) with the further-restriction maps, prior to the \(R(U)\)-module refinement.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean; functoriality is \(\mathtt{restrictionMap\_ id}\) / \(\mathtt{restrictionMap\_ comp}\).

Definition 545 Evaluation functional of a dual section
#

Uses

  • Definition 493
  • Definition 493

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.evalLin

At an object \(X\), the \(R(X)\)-linear map \(M(X) \to R(X)\) obtained by evaluating a dual section \(\varphi \) at its terminal component.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, as the underlying linear map of the terminal component of \(\varphi \).

Lemma 546 Additivity of the evaluation functional
#

Uses

  • Definition 545
  • Definition 545

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.evalLin_add

\(\mathtt{evalLin}\) is additive in the dual section \(\varphi \).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, the categorical \(\mathrm{Hom}\)-addition being applied sectionwise.

Lemma 547 Linearity of the evaluation functional
#

Uses

  • Definition 545
  • Lemma 527
  • Definition 545
  • Lemma 527

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.evalLin_smul

\(\mathtt{evalLin}\) is \(R(X)\)-linear in \(\varphi \), using the morphism-module action and the terminal ring map.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, via \(c \cdot \varphi = \varphi \circ m_c\) and \(\mathtt{termRingMap\_ terminal}\).

Definition 548 Open-by-open evaluation/contraction map
#

Uses

  • Definition 545
  • Lemma 546
  • Lemma 547
  • Definition 545
  • Lemma 546
  • Lemma 547

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.internalHomEvalApp

At an object \(X\), the \(R(X)\)-bilinear contraction \((M(X)) \otimes _{R(X)} (M|_X \to R|_X) \to R(X)\), \(s \otimes \varphi \mapsto \varphi (s)\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, lifting the bilinear contraction through the tensor product; bilinearity is \(\mathtt{evalLin\_ add}\) / \(\mathtt{evalLin\_ smul}\).

Lemma 549 Value of the contraction on a simple tensor
#

Uses

  • Definition 548
  • Definition 548

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.internalHomEvalApp_tmul

The contraction sends \(s \otimes \varphi \) to \(\mathtt{evalLin}\, \varphi \, s\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, by definitional unfolding.

Lemma 550 Further restriction along the canonical over-morphism
#

Uses

  • Definition 534
  • Definition 534

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.restr_map_homMk

For \(f : X \to Y\), further-restricting \(M|_{X}\) along the canonical over-category morphism is definitionally \(M.\mathtt{map}\, f\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, by definitional unfolding (extracted to keep its \(\mathtt{whnf}\) cost local).

Lemma 551 Sectionwise applied form of the pushforward natural transformation
#

Uses

  • Lemma 408
  • Lemma 408

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans_app_app_apply

The component of \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\, \varphi \, \alpha \) at \(M\), \(U\) acts on a section as \(M.\mathtt{map}\, (\alpha _U)^{\mathrm{op}}\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, by definitional unfolding.

Lemma 552 Sectionwise form of the pushforward congruence (forward)
#

Uses

  • Lemma 408
  • Lemma 408

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app

The forward component of \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\, e\) acts as the identity on sections.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, by substituting the equality \(e\).

Lemma 553 Sectionwise form of the pushforward congruence (inverse)
#

Uses

  • Lemma 408
  • Lemma 408

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app

The inverse component of \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\, e\) acts as the identity on sections.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, by substituting the equality \(e\).

Definition 554 Lax-monoidal unit of restrict-scalars
#

Uses

  • Lemma 406
  • Lemma 406

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxε

The lax-monoidal unit \(\varepsilon \) of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \alpha \), assembled sectionwise from the \(\mathbf{ModuleCat}\)-level lax units.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, sectionwise from \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\)’s lax unit.

Definition 555 Lax-monoidal tensorator of restrict-scalars
#

Uses

  • Lemma 406
  • Lemma 406

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxμ

The lax-monoidal tensorator \(\mu \) of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \alpha \), assembled sectionwise from the \(\mathbf{ModuleCat}\)-level lax tensorators.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, sectionwise from \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\)’s lax tensorator.

Lemma 556 Ring-iso base-change equivalence on a simple tensor
#

Uses

  • Lemma 409
  • Lemma 409

Lean declarations

  • restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv_apply_tmul

The forward map of \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}\, e\) sends \(a \otimes _R b\) to \(a \otimes _S b\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, unfolding the linear equivalence on a simple tensor.

20.13.3 Vestigial / off-path helper declarations

The following are the off-critical-path Lean helper declarations supporting the flat-whiskering localizer stability 412, the flatness-free whiskering 416, the \(R_x\)-linear stalk map 414, the stalkwise localizer characterisation 427 and the shared slice-site equivalence 500. Each is proved directly in Lean.

Lemma 557 Sectionwise left-whiskering on a simple tensor
#

Uses

  • Lemma 412
  • Definition 403
  • Lemma 412
  • Definition 403

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul

The underlying additive map of \((F \triangleleft g)_X\) sends \(a \otimes b\) to \(a \otimes g_X(b)\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, as the action of \(\mathtt{LinearMap.lTensor}\) on a simple tensor.

Lemma 558 Sectionwise left-whiskering is a tensor map
#

Uses

  • Lemma 557
  • Lemma 557

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply

The underlying additive map of \((F \triangleleft g)_X\) is \(\mathtt{LinearMap.lTensor}\, (F(X))\, g_X\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, identifying the whiskered map with the left-tensor map.

Lemma 559 Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering
#

Uses

  • Lemma 557
  • Lemma 412
  • Lemma 557
  • Lemma 412

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft

If \(g\) is locally surjective then so is \(F \triangleleft g\), with no flatness hypothesis (pure right-exactness of \(\otimes \)).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, lifting a local preimage of \(b\) to \(a \otimes b\).

Lemma 560 Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering
#

Uses

  • Lemma 558
  • Lemma 412
  • Lemma 558
  • Lemma 412

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat

For sectionwise-flat \(F\), if \(g\) is locally injective then so is \(F \triangleleft g\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, via \(\mathtt{Module.Flat.lTensor\_ exact}\) on the kernel exact sequence and local injectivity of \(g\) on each simple tensor.

Lemma 561 Flat right-whiskering preserves the localizer
#

Uses

  • Lemma 412
  • Lemma 412

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_flat

For sectionwise-flat \(F\) and \(g \in J.W\), the right-whiskered \(g \triangleright F\) again lies in \(J.W\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, conjugating the left-whiskered statement by the braiding.

Lemma 562 Flatness-free right-whiskering preserves the localizer
#

Uses

  • Lemma 416
  • Lemma 416

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W

For arbitrary \(F\) and a locally bijective \(g \in J.W\), the right-whiskered \(g \triangleright F\) again lies in \(J.W\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, conjugating the flatness-free left-whiskered statement by the braiding.

Lemma 563 Germ characterisation of the linear stalk map
#

Uses

  • Lemma 414
  • Lemma 414

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearMap_germ

On the germ of a section \(s\) over \(U\), \(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap}\, g\, x\) is the germ of \(g_U(s)\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, the defining naturality of the stalk map.

Lemma 564 A stalkwise-iso morphism is bijective on stalks
#

Uses

  • Lemma 414
  • Lemma 414

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso

If the underlying \(\mathbf{Ab}\)-stalk map of \(g\) at \(x\) is an isomorphism then \(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap}\, g\, x\) is bijective.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, from \(\mathtt{ConcreteCategory.bijective\_ of\_ isIso}\).

Definition 565 Linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism
#

Uses

  • Lemma 414
  • Lemma 564
  • Lemma 414
  • Lemma 564

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso

When the \(\mathbf{Ab}\)-stalk map of \(g\) at \(x\) is an isomorphism, \(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap}\, g\, x\) bundles to an \(R_x\)-linear equivalence \(M_x \xrightarrow {\sim } N_x\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, via \(\mathtt{LinearEquiv.ofBijective}\).

Lemma 566 Injective on stalks implies locally injective
#

Uses

  • Lemma 427
  • Lemma 427

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.isLocallyInjective_of_injective_stalk

For a morphism of presheaves valued in a concrete category over a topological space, stalkwise injectivity implies local injectivity for the topological Grothendieck topology.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, through \(\mathtt{germ\_ eq}\): agreeing germs agree on a neighbourhood, which covers each point.

Lemma 567 Locally injective implies injective on stalks
#

Uses

  • Lemma 427
  • Lemma 427

Lean declarations

  • PresheafOfModules.injective_stalk_of_isLocallyInjective

The converse: a locally injective morphism of presheaves over a topological space induces injective stalk maps.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, via \(\mathtt{germ\_ eq}\) and the covering of the equalizer sieve.

Lemma 568 Pointwise cover-correspondence across the open embedding
#

Uses

  • Lemma 500
  • Lemma 500

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overEquiv_image_cover_iff

A sieve \(S\) on \(W : \mathtt{Opens}\, \widetilde{U}\) is a covering sieve in the subspace topology iff its pushforward along the open-embedding image functor \(\widetilde{U} \hookrightarrow X\) is a covering sieve in \(X\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: both sides are the pointwise neighbourhood-cover condition, matched across the injection \(\mathtt{Subtype.val}\).

Definition 569 Dense-subsite datum for the slice-site equivalence
#

Uses

  • Lemma 500
  • Lemma 568
  • Lemma 500
  • Lemma 568

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite

The inverse of \(\mathtt{overEquivalence}\, U\) exhibits the opens of the subspace \(\widetilde{U}\) as a dense subsite of the slice site over \(U\); the only non-formal field is the pointwise cover-correspondence.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, discharging \(\mathtt{functorPushforward\_ mem\_ iff}\) by \(\mathtt{overEquiv\_ image\_ cover\_ iff}\).

20.14 Substrate helper declarations (wired lean-aux blocks)

This section gives a blueprint entry to each of the project-local helper declarations of TensorObjSubstrate.lean that the substrate construction and the pullback–tensor monoidality machinery invoke internally. Each had been written only as an inline mid-proof \(\mathtt{\backslash lean}\) reference of an existing block, which leaves it unregistered as a graph node; here each receives its own \(\mathtt{\backslash label}\) and its \(\mathtt{\backslash uses}\) edges are read off its Lean body, and the consumers above are wired to depend on them. All but one are sorry-free; the single residual is 593, whose informal proof is given in full.

Definition 570 Substrate \(\otimes \) respects a pair of isomorphisms
#

Uses

  • Definition 403
  • Definition 403

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso

For \(M \cong M'\) and \(N \cong N'\) in \(\mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\), sheafifying the presheaf-of-modules tensor of the underlying presheaf isomorphisms produces an isomorphism \(M \otimes _X N \cong M' \otimes _X N'\). This is the functor-of-isomorphisms form of the substrate tensor, used to build the middle-four interchange and the well-definedness of the multiplication on iso-classes.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: it is \(\mathtt{sheafification}.\mathrm{mapIso}\) applied to the monoidal \(\mathtt{tensorIso}\) of the two forgetful images of the given isomorphisms.

Definition 571 \(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes _X \mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_X\)
#

Uses

  • Definition 403
  • Definition 403

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_unit_iso

The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure sheaf: \(\mathtt{tensorObj}\, \mathcal{O}_X\, \mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), where \(\mathcal{O}_X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\, X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\). It is the unit isomorphism consumed by the invertibility constructions.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: the sheafification of the presheaf left unitor \(\lambda _{\mathbf{1}}\), composed with the sheafification-adjunction counit at the already-sheaf unit.

Definition 572 Middle-four interchange for \(\otimes _X\)
#

Uses

  • Lemma 418
  • Lemma 420
  • Definition 570
  • Lemma 418
  • Lemma 420
  • Definition 570

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_middleFour

For arbitrary \(A,B,C,D \in \mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\) there is an isomorphism \((A \otimes B) \otimes (C \otimes D) \cong (A \otimes C) \otimes (B \otimes D)\), reassociating the four factors. It is the bookkeeping isomorphism used to show \(\otimes \)-invertibility is closed under tensor product.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: an assembly of the unconditional associator 418 and the braiding 420 through \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\); no coherence pentagon is consumed.

Definition 573 Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open
#

Uses

  • Lemma 465
  • Lemma 465

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictIsoUnitOfLE

If \(M\) is trivialised on an open \(U\) (i.e. \(M|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\)) then it is trivialised on every open \(W \le U\): \(M|_W \cong \mathcal{O}_W\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: factor \(W \hookrightarrow X\) through \(W \hookrightarrow U\), transport the given trivialisation along the open immersion via the restriction/pullback comparison functors, and identify the restricted unit with the unit through the pullback unit comparison 465 (an isomorphism because the open immersion’s \(\mathtt{Opens.map}\) is \(\mathtt{Final}\)).

Lemma 574 Underlying presheaf map of a promoted module morphism
#

Uses

  • Definition 403
  • Definition 403

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf_map_homMk

The underlying \(\mathtt{Ab}\)-presheaf morphism of a module morphism promoted from a sectionwise-\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear presheaf map \(g\) is \(g\) itself. This is the round-trip identity used by the gluing descent of 421, where local contraction isomorphisms are glued to a global module morphism.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by \(\mathtt{rfl}\): promotion wraps \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.homMk}\, g\) and the underlying-presheaf projection unwraps it.

Lemma 575 Unit comparison is an iso from a finality hypothesis
#

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_pbu_of_final

For \(g : Y \to X\) with \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\, g.\mathtt{base}).\mathtt{Final}\), the Mathlib unit comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\, g\) is an isomorphism.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by \(\mathtt{inferInstance}\): the lone finality hypothesis is isolated at a clean site so the Mathlib \(\mathtt{OfFinal}\) instance synthesises without colliding with other in-scope \(\mathtt{Final}\)/\(\mathtt{IsIso}\) hypotheses.

Definition 576 Bundled iso form of the unit comparison
#

Uses

  • Lemma 575
  • Lemma 575

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso

For \(g : Y \to X\) with \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\, g.\mathtt{base}).\mathtt{Final}\), the isomorphism \(g^*\mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\) bundling the unit comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\, g\) of 575. Handing out the bundled \(\mathtt{Iso}\) keeps downstream reasoning at the \(\mathtt{Iso}\) level.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: \(\mathtt{asIso}\) of the comparison with the explicit witness 575.

Lemma 577 \(\mathtt{hom}\) of the bundled unit comparison
#

Uses

  • Definition 576
  • Definition 576

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso_hom

The \(\mathtt{hom}\) component of \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso}\, g\) is the bare unit comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\, g\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by \(\mathtt{rfl}\) (\(\mathtt{asIso}\) exposes its underlying map).

Definition 578 Sheafification–monoidality reconciliation brick
#

Uses

  • Lemma 413
  • Lemma 416
  • Lemma 413
  • Lemma 416

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafifyTensorUnitIso

For presheaves of modules \(P,Q\) on \(X\), sheafifying the presheaf tensor \(P \otimes Q\) agrees with sheafifying the tensor of the underlying presheaves of their sheafifications. This is the “sheafification is monoidal” reconciliation bridging a presheaf-level tensorator with the substrate \(\otimes _X\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean, exactly as in 418: whisker the sheafification unit \(\eta \) (a \(J.W\)-morphism) on each side and invert under sheafification via 413 together with the flatness-free whiskering stability 416.

Lemma 579 \(\mathtt{hom}\) of the reconciliation brick as a whisker composite
#

Uses

  • Definition 578
  • Definition 578

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_eq

The \(\mathtt{hom}\) of 578 equals the sheafification of \(\eta _P \rhd Q\) followed by the sheafification of \((aP).\mathrm{val} \lhd \eta _Q\), stated on the common \(\circ \, \mathtt{forget}_2\) carrier so the two whisker factors can be merged.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by \(\mathtt{rfl}\), stripping the carrier cast.

Lemma 580 \(\mathtt{hom}\) of the reconciliation brick as one \(\mathtt{tensorHom}\)
#

Uses

  • Lemma 579
  • Definition 578
  • Lemma 579

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_eq'

The \(\mathtt{hom}\) of 578 equals the sheafification of the single \(\mathtt{tensorHom}\) \(\eta _P \otimes \eta _Q\) of the two sheafification-unit components — keeping every term in one monoidal instance so naturality reduces to plain bifunctoriality.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: merge the two whisker factors of 579 by \(\mathtt{\leftarrow Functor.map\_ comp}\) and identify with \(\mathtt{tensorHom\_ def}\).

Definition 581 Codomain unit reconciliation \(a_Y\, \mathbf{1} \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\)
#

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafifyUnitIso

The sheafification counit identifies the sheafified presheaf monoidal unit \(a_Y.\mathrm{obj}\, \mathbf{1}\) with the sheaf-level structure module \(\mathcal{O}_Y\). It is the right-hand vertical of the \(\eta \)-bridge square of 472.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: the unit \(\mathbf{1} = (\mathtt{unit}\, Y).\mathrm{val}\) is already a sheaf, so the sheafification-adjunction counit at it is an isomorphism, taken via \(\mathtt{asIso}\).

Definition 582 Sheafified presheaf-pullback \(\cong \) abstract pullback on a value
#

Uses

  • Lemma 474
  • Lemma 474

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackValIso

For \(f : Y \to X\) and \(M \in \mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\), sheafifying the presheaf-level pullback of the underlying presheaf \(M.\mathrm{val}\) recovers the abstract \(\mathcal{O}\)-module pullback \((\mathtt{pullback}\, f).\mathrm{obj}\, M\). It reconciles the right-hand side of \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) with the substrate tensor of the pullbacks.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: the inverse of \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) (474) at \(M.\mathrm{val}\), composed with the pullback of the sheafification counit at the already-sheaf \(M\).

Definition 583 Topological inverse image \(\mathtt{pullback0}\)
#

Uses

  • Lemma 596
  • Lemma 596

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullback0

The left adjoint of the fixed-ring pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\, F\, R\); on underlying presheaves it is the left Kan extension along \(F^{\mathrm{op}}\). A project-local carrier of the pullback Lan decomposition.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\, F\, R\) is definitionally a pushforward hence a right adjoint (596), and \(\mathtt{pullback0}\) is its \(\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\).

Definition 584 Adjunction \(\mathtt{pullback0} \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}_0\)
#

Uses

  • Definition 583
  • Lemma 596
  • Definition 583
  • Lemma 596

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullback0Adjunction

The adjunction exhibiting 583 as the left adjoint of the fixed-ring pushforward.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by \(\mathtt{Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint}\) of the pushforward (596).

Lemma 585 The sheafified \(\otimes \) of the two \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) is an iso
#

Uses

  • Definition 582
  • Definition 403
  • Definition 582

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso

The fourth factor of \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) — the sheafification of the \(\mathtt{tensorHom}\) of the two \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) comparisons (one for \(M\), one for \(N\)) — is an isomorphism.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: it is the \(\mathtt{hom}\) of the sheafification \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) of the monoidal \(\mathtt{tensorIso}\) of the two \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) isomorphisms.

Lemma 586 Converse of \(\mathtt{isIso\_ sheafification\_ map\_ of\_ W}\)
#

Uses

  • Lemma 413
  • Lemma 413

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.W_of_isIso_sheafification

A morphism of presheaves of modules whose image under the associated-sheaf functor is an isomorphism lies, on underlying additive presheaves, in the sheafification localizer \(J.W\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: it is the morphism-property identity “the sheafification functor is the localization at \(J.W.\mathtt{inverseImage}\)” (413) read backwards.

Lemma 587 \(\delta \)-wrapping on the unit pair from the \(\eta \)-bridge
#

Uses

  • Lemma 586
  • Lemma 416
  • Lemma 413
  • Lemma 586
  • Lemma 416
  • Lemma 413

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_sheafifyDelta_unitPair_of_isIso_sheafifyEta

If the sheafified presheaf unit comparison \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}\, (\eta \, (\mathtt{pullback}\, \varphi '))\) is an isomorphism, then so is the sheafified oplax cotensorator \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}\, \delta \) on the unit pair \((\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: the oplax left-unitality \(\mathtt{left\_ unitality\_ hom}\) factors \(\delta \, \mathbf{1}\, \mathbf{1}\) through the whiskered \(\eta \) and the (iso) unitor; sheafifying, the whiskered \(\eta \)-factor is an iso because a sheafified iso lies in \(J.W\) (586), \(J.W\) is stable under right-whiskering (416), and \(J.W\)-morphisms sheafify to isos (413).

Lemma 588 \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) on the unit pair from the \(\eta \)-bridge
#

Uses

  • Lemma 587
  • Lemma 462
  • Lemma 587
  • Lemma 462

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_pullbackTensorMap_unitPair_of_isIso_sheafifyEta

If the sheafified presheaf unit comparison \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}\, (\eta \, (\mathtt{pullback}\, \varphi '))\) is an isomorphism, then \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\, f\, \mathcal{O}_X\, \mathcal{O}_X\) is an isomorphism.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: chain the \(\delta \)-wrapping 587 into the reduction brick 462 on \(M = N = \mathcal{O}\).

Lemma 589 Composite ring-map reconciliation
#

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.toRingCatSheafHom_comp_hom_reconcile

For composable \(h : Z \to Y\), \(f : Y \to X\), the structure ring-presheaf map of the composite \(h \circ f\) factors as \(\varphi '_f\) followed by the whiskered \(\varphi '_h\). This feeds the presheaf \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) into the oplax \(\mathtt{comp\_ }\delta \) decomposition (Sq2 of 478).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by \(\mathtt{rfl}\): the two sides hold up to definitional equality at default transparency.

Lemma 590 Sq2b — monoidality of the presheaf \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)
#

Uses

  • Lemma 457
  • Lemma 456
  • Lemma 605
  • Lemma 457
  • Lemma 456

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp_δ

The oplax cotensorator \(\delta \) of the pullback for a composite ring map transports, through the pullback pseudofunctor coherence \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\), into the \(\mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.comp}\) cotensorator of the composite pullback. This is the \(\eta \to \delta \) analogue, at the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) level, of the unit coherence 464.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by the adjunction-mate calculus: transpose under the pullback–pushforward adjunction, rewrite the oplax \(\delta \) as the mate of the lax \(\mu \) of the pushforward (456), and use the conjugate identity for \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}.\mathrm{inv}\) (here \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp} = \mathtt{Iso.refl}\)); the sole residual is the lax-\(\mu \) composition coherence \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp\_ lax\_ }\mu \), discharged by a sectionwise pure-tensor collapse.

Lemma 591 Sheaf-level conjugate of \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}.\mathrm{inv}\)
#

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheaf_unit_comp_pushforward_pullbackComp_inv

For composable \(h : Z \to Y\), \(f : Y \to X\) and \(Q \in \mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\), the unit of the composite-pullback adjunction post-composed with the pushforward of \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}.\mathrm{inv}\) equals the unit of the composite of the \(f\)- and \(h\)-adjunctions post-composed with \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}.\mathrm{hom}\). It is the cheap, sheafification-free R0-peel piece of the Sq1 mate calculus.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: the sheaf-level instance of \(\mathtt{unit\_ conjugateEquiv}\) combined with \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_ pullbackComp\_ inv}\) (the mate of \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}.\mathrm{inv}\) is \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}.\mathrm{hom}\)).

Lemma 592 Sq1 — composition coherence of \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)
#

Uses

  • Lemma 474
  • Lemma 591
  • Lemma 593
  • Lemma 474
  • Lemma 591
  • Lemma 593

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafificationCompPullback_comp

For composable \(h : Z \to Y\), \(f : Y \to X\) and a presheaf \(P\) over \(X\), the sheafification–pullback comparison of \(h \circ f\) factors through the comparisons of \(f\) and \(h\), conjugated by the sheaf-level pullback pseudofunctor iso \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\, h\, f\) and the sheafified presheaf pullback pseudofunctor iso. This is the \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) twin of 464; it is the S1-foundational coherence consumed by 478.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: both \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) isos are \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) of composite adjunctions (474); transpose the identity under the composite adjunction for \(h \circ f\), evaluate the left-hand side by \(\mathtt{homEquiv\_ leftAdjointUniq\_ hom\_ app}\), peel the leading \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}.\mathrm{inv}\) factor by 591, and discharge the residual unit identity by 593.

Lemma 593 Sq1 tail — the R1/R5 unit collapse
#

Uses

  • Lemma 474
  • Lemma 476
  • Lemma 464
  • Lemma 599
  • Lemma 606
  • Lemma 474
  • Lemma 476
  • Lemma 464
  • Lemma 599
  • Lemma 606

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafificationCompPullback_comp_tail

Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference. After the R0-peel of 592, the residual obligation is the unit identity for the two-layer composite adjunction (presheaf pullback–pushforward composed with sheafification). Writing \(B_\varphi \) for that sheaf-level composite adjunction, \(\mathtt{PresheafPullback}_f\) for the presheaf-level pullback along \(f\), \(R_1 = h^*\bigl((\mathtt{sheafCompPb}\, f).\mathrm{app}\, P\bigr)\) and \(R_5 = (\mathtt{sheafCompPb}\, h).\mathrm{app}\, (\mathtt{PresheafPullback}_f\, P)\) for the two sub-comparison factors, the tail asserts that the composite unit \(B_{h \circ f}.\mathrm{unit}.\mathrm{app}\, P\) equals the sheafification-unit of \(X\) followed by the (forgetful, change-of-rings-along-identity) image of the merged factor \(\bigl((B_f \mathbin {.\mathrm{comp}} B_h).\mathrm{unit} \mathbin {;} \mathtt{pushforwardComp}.\mathrm{hom}\bigr) \mathbin {;} (\text{pushforward of } R_1 \mathbin {;} R_5 \mathbin {;} a_Z.\mathrm{map}\, \delta _{\mathrm{pre}})\).

Proof ▶

Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.

This is the sheafification-laden analogue of the unit composition coherence 464, one sheafification layer up. There the adjunctions are single transposes and the coherence is sectionwise definitional; here \(B_f\), \(B_h\) are composite adjunctions \((\text{presheaf pullback--pushforward})\mathbin {.\mathrm{comp}} (\text{sheafification})\), so the coherence is a genuine mate-calculus identity rather than a reflexivity.

Both \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) isos are \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) of these composite adjunctions (474). The strategy is to keep the left-hand composite unit \(B_{h \circ f}.\mathrm{unit}.\mathrm{app}\, P\) intact and assemble the right-hand side into it, in the following ordered steps.

(a) Strip the identity wrapper. The tail goal carries an outer \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}(\mathbf{1})\) change-of-rings factor from the identity ring map; it is removed first (restriction of scalars along the identity is the identity functor), exposing the genuine comparison factors.

(b) Distribute the right-hand sheaf composite under \(\mathtt{forget}\). Pushing the forgetful functor \(\mathtt{forget}\) (sheaf \(\to \) presheaf) through the right-hand composite separates the two sub-comparison factors \(R_1\) (the \(f\)-layer) and \(R_5\) (the \(h\)-layer), confined to the right-hand side so that the left-hand composite unit \(B_{h \circ f}.\mathrm{unit}\) is left intact for the final reassembly. (Distributing without confinement would unfold the left-hand sheafification unit into its \(\mathtt{toSheafify} \mathbin {;} \mathtt{restrictHomEquiv}\) expansion, the wrong normal form.)

(c) Recover the sub-comparison units. Apply the \(P\)-general uniqueness-of-left-adjoints brick 476 (\(\mathtt{homEquiv\_ leftAdjointUniq\_ hom\_ app}\) in its \(P\)-general form) to rewrite \(R_1\) as the composite-adjunction unit \(B_f.\mathrm{unit}.\mathrm{app}\, P\) and \(R_5\) as \(B_h.\mathrm{unit}.\mathrm{app}\, (\mathtt{PresheafPullback}_f\, P)\). This is the load-bearing step: it identifies each \(\mathtt{sheafCompPb}\) factor with a unit of the appropriate composite adjunction.

(d) Slide \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) past the recovered units. The sheaf-level comparison \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\, h\, f\) is moved across the two recovered units \(B_f.\mathrm{unit}\), \(B_h.\mathrm{unit}\) by its naturality.

(e) Reassemble the composite unit. Expanding \(B_{h \circ f}.\mathrm{unit}\) by the composite-adjunction unit formula \(\mathtt{Adjunction.comp\_ unit\_ app}\) and applying \(\mathtt{Adjunction.unit\_ naturality}\) identifies the slid expression of step (d) with the left-hand composite unit, closing the tail.

The single non-mechanical bridge is the presheaf\(\leftrightarrow \)sheaf pushforward compatibility \(\mathtt{forget} \circ \mathtt{pushforward}^{\mathrm{sheaf}} = \mathtt{pushforward}^{\mathrm{pre}} \circ \mathtt{forget}\) (a definitional identity, since the sheaf pushforward is built sectionwise from the presheaf pushforward and \(\mathtt{forget}\) is the underlying-presheaf projection), interacting with the recovered \(B_f\)- and \(B_h\)-units of step (c); once it is in hand, steps (a)–(e) are a mechanical paste. This identity reduces, exactly as the unit twin 464 does, to \(\mathtt{Iso.inv\_ hom\_ id}\) / unit-coherence at the unit presheaf. Warning: transposing the whole tail back under the composite adjunction — instead of recovering the two sub-units individually in step (c) — is circular: it returns the very identity one set out to prove.

Lemma 594 Y-side sheafification right-triangle for the oplax unit comparison
#

Uses

  • Definition 581
  • Definition 581

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackSheafifyUnitEtaTriangle

For \(f : Y \to X\) with \(\varphi ' = (\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\, f).\mathrm{hom}\) and \(F = \mathtt{pullback}\, \varphi '\), the sheafification unit at \(F.\mathrm{obj}\, \mathbf{1}\), post-composed with the underlying presheaf maps of \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}\, (\eta \, F)\) and \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom}\), recovers the oplax unit comparison \(\eta \, F\). It is a substep of the unit square 472.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: this is \(\mathtt{Equiv.apply\_ symm\_ apply}\) for the presheaf sheafification adjunction \(\mathtt{homEquiv}\) — the second factor is \(\mathtt{homEquiv}^{-1}(\eta \, F)\), its counit leg being the adjunction counit \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom}\) (581) at the sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_Y\) — discharged by sheafification-unit naturality and the right triangle.

20.14.1 Change-of-rings, lax-\(\mu \) and \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\) helpers

This subsection blueprints the change-of-base-ring cluster of TensorObjSubstrate.lean: the extension-of-scalars functor and its adjunction, the lax-monoidal structure maps \(\mu \) of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforward}\), and the monoidality of the pushforward composition cell (\(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\)) — the change-of-rings coherence that is the sole residual of the presheaf-level Sq2b lemma 590. Each helper is sorry-free; its \(\mathtt{\backslash uses}\) edges are read off its Lean body.

Lemma 595 \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) is a right adjoint
#

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint

For a morphism of ring presheaves \(\varphi : S \to F^{\mathrm{op}} \! \cdot \! R\), the restriction-of-scalars functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \varphi \) on presheaves of modules is a right adjoint. This packages the existence of its left adjoint \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\, \varphi \).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \varphi \) is definitionally the fixed-target pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\, (F = \mathbf{1})\, \varphi \), whose right-adjointness is the ambient Mathlib instance; conclude by \(\mathtt{inferInstanceAs}\).

Lemma 596 \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\) is a right adjoint
#

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint

The fixed-ring presheaf pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\, F\, R\) is a right adjoint; this carries the existence of the topological inverse image \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\, F\, R\) is definitionally \(\mathtt{pushforward}\, (\mathbf{1}\, (F^{\mathrm{op}} \! \cdot \! R))\) (restriction of scalars along the identity is the identity functor), whose right-adjointness is the ambient Mathlib instance; conclude by \(\mathtt{inferInstanceAs}\).

Definition 597 Extension of scalars \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\)
#

Uses

  • Lemma 595
  • Lemma 595

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.extendScalars

For \(\varphi : S \to F^{\mathrm{op}} \! \cdot \! R\), the extension-of-scalars functor \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\, \varphi := (\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \varphi ) .\mathrm{leftAdjoint}\), the left adjoint of restriction of scalars along \(\varphi \). It is the carrier of the strong-monoidal change-of-base on presheaves of modules.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: the left adjoint exists because \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \varphi \) is a right adjoint (595).

Definition 598 The adjunction \(\mathtt{extendScalars} \dashv \mathtt{restrictScalars}\)
#

Uses

  • Definition 597
  • Lemma 595
  • Definition 597
  • Lemma 595

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.extendScalarsAdjunction

The adjunction \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\, \varphi \dashv \mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \varphi \) witnessing extension of scalars as left adjoint to restriction of scalars.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: \(\mathtt{Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint}\) applied to \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \varphi \) (595).

Lemma 599 \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}(\mathbf{1})\) on morphisms
#

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictScalarsId_map

For a morphism \(g\) of presheaves of modules over \(R\), \((\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, (\mathbf{1}\, R)).\mathrm{map}\, g = g\): restriction of scalars along the identity ring map acts as the identity on morphisms.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by \(\mathtt{rfl}\): \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, (\mathbf{1})\) is definitionally the identity functor, stated as a propositional rewrite so the wrapper can be stripped syntactically without a whole-term \(\mathtt{whnf}\).

Lemma 600 Sectionwise value of the \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) tensorator \(\mu \)
#

Uses

  • Definition 555
  • Definition 555

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictScalars_μ_app

The lax tensorator \(\mu \) of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \alpha \), evaluated at a section \(W\), is the \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) lax \(\mu \) of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, (\alpha _W)\) on the sections. It exposes the ambient presheaf \(\mu \) in a form on which the pure-tensor rule matches syntactically.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by \(\mathtt{rfl}\): the presheaf tensorator \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsLax}\mu \) (555) is defined sectionwise as the \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) tensorator.

Lemma 601 Pure-tensor value of the \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) tensorator
#

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.forget₂_restrictScalars_μ_hom_tmul

On a pure tensor \(m \otimes _t n\), the \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) lax tensorator \(\mu \) of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, f\) (with \(\mathtt{forget}_2\)-carrier rings coming from presheaves of commutative rings) is the identity: \(\mu (m \otimes _t n) = m \otimes _t n\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: it is \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars\_ }\mu \mathtt{\_ tmul}\), the Mathlib pure-tensor formula for the change-of-rings tensorator, with the \(\mathtt{CommRing}\) instances supplied by the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) carriers.

Lemma 602 Pure-tensor value of the presheaf \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) tensorator
#

Uses

  • Lemma 600
  • Lemma 600

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictScalars_μ_app_tmul

On a pure tensor, \((\mu (\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \alpha )\, M_1\, M_2)_W (m \otimes _t n) = m \otimes _t n\): the presheaf \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) tensorator is the identity on pure tensors.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: expose the \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) tensorator by 600, then apply the Mathlib pure-tensor rule \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars\_ }\mu \mathtt{\_ tmul}\).

Lemma 603 Pure-tensor value of the \(\mathtt{pushforward}\)-mapped \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) tensorator
#

Uses

  • Lemma 602
  • Lemma 602

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforward_map_restrictScalars_μ_app_tmul

The outer leg of 605: applied to a pure tensor, \(((\mathtt{pushforward}\, \varphi ).\mathrm{map}\, (\mu (\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \psi ) \, N_1\, N_2))_W\) is the identity.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: reindex the section map through \(\mathtt{pushforward\_ map\_ app\_ apply}\) (\(\mathtt{pushforward}\, \varphi \) is \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0 \cdot \mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \varphi \), so the section map at \(W\) is the \(\mu \) at \(F^{\mathrm{op}}.\mathrm{obj}\, W\)), then collapse by 602.

Lemma 604 Reduction of the \(\mathtt{pushforward}\) tensorator to the \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) \(\mu \)
#

Uses

  • Lemma 456
  • Definition 555
  • Lemma 456
  • Definition 555

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforward_μ_eq

The lax tensorator \(\mu \) of a single \(\mathtt{pushforward}\, \varphi \) equals the lax tensorator of the change-of-rings \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\, \varphi '\) on the (strongly-monoidal, \(\mu _{\mathrm{Iso}} = \mathbf{1}\)) reindexed objects \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}}\, F\, R_0\). This unfolds the opaque pushforward lax-monoidal \(\mu \) into the directly computable \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) \(\mu \).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by \(\mathtt{rfl}\): the pushforward lax-monoidal structure (456) is the \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) of the (identity) \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\) tensorator with the \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) tensorator (555), which is definitionally the right-hand side.

Lemma 605 Sq2b residual — monoidality of the \(\mathtt{pushforward}\) composition cell
#

Uses

  • Lemma 604
  • Lemma 600
  • Lemma 601
  • Lemma 603
  • Lemma 604
  • Lemma 600
  • Lemma 601
  • Lemma 603

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp_lax_μ

The change-of-rings coherence “\(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) is monoidal”: the lax tensorator \(\mu \) of the composite pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\, \psi \cdot \mathtt{pushforward}\, \varphi \) (built by \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\)) agrees with the lax tensorator of the single pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\, (\varphi \, ;\, F^{\mathrm{op}} \! \lhd \! \psi )\). It is the sole genuine residual of the presheaf-level Sq2b lemma 590.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by a sectionwise pure-tensor collapse: \(\mathtt{hom\_ ext}\) then \(\mathtt{tensor\_ ext}\), unfold the composite \(\mu \) by \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp\_ }\mu \), lighten each \(\mu (\mathtt{pushforward}\, \_ )\) to a \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) \(\mu \) by 604, expose the \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) \(\mu \) by 600, and collapse the inner leg by 601 and the outer \((\mathtt{pushforward}\, \varphi ).\mathrm{map}\) leg by 603; the two collapsed pure tensors are then definitionally equal. (The equality is genuinely not \(\mathtt{rfl}\) at the presheaf level — the \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) \(\mu \) on a pure tensor is real base-change content — so the atomic-object helpers are applied as explicit arguments and matched by \(\mathtt{erw}\) to avoid a \(\mathtt{whnf}\)-explosion.)

Lemma 606 Forgetful image of a sheaf-pushforward map
#

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.forget_map_pushforward_map

The forgetful functor \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.forget}\) intertwines the sheaf-level \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforward}\, \varphi \) with the presheaf-level \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pushforward}\, \varphi .\mathrm{hom}\): for a morphism \(g\) of sheaves of modules, \(\mathtt{forget}.\mathrm{map}\, ((\mathtt{pushforward}\, \varphi ) .\mathrm{map}\, g) = (\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pushforward}\, \varphi .\mathrm{hom}) .\mathrm{map}\, (\mathtt{forget}.\mathrm{map}\, g)\). This is the STEP-1 binding reconciliation of the Sq1 tail.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by \(\mathtt{rfl}\): the sheaf pushforward is built sectionwise from the presheaf pushforward and \(\mathtt{forget}\) is the underlying-presheaf projection, so the two sides are definitionally equal.

20.14.2 The Picard quotient: setoid, multiplication and inverse

This subsection blueprints the three quotient-level helpers underlying the invertibility-carrier Picard group 483 and its abelian-group law 487: the isomorphism setoid, the tensor-induced multiplication, and the dual-induced inverse on iso-classes.

Definition 607 The isomorphism setoid of invertible modules
#

Uses

  • Definition 454
  • Definition 454

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.picSetoid

The setoid on \(\otimes \)-invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules with \(M \sim M'\) iff there is an isomorphism \(M \cong M'\). The quotient of this setoid is the carrier of the Picard group \(\operatorname{Pic}X\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean: reflexivity, symmetry and transitivity of the \(\mathtt{Nonempty}\, (M \cong M')\) relation are witnessed by \(\mathtt{Iso.refl}\), \(\mathtt{Iso.symm}\) and \(\mathtt{Iso.trans}\).

Definition 608 Multiplication on \(\operatorname{Pic}X\) induced by \(\otimes _X\)
#

Uses

  • Definition 607
  • Definition 483
  • Definition 570
  • Lemma 484
  • Definition 607
  • Definition 483
  • Definition 570
  • Lemma 484

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.picMul

The multiplication \([M] \cdot [M'] := [M \otimes _X M']\) on \(\operatorname{Pic}X\), well-defined on iso-classes.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by \(\mathtt{Quotient.lift}_2\): the value lands in \(\operatorname{Pic}X\) because the tensor of invertibles is invertible (484), and it respects the setoid because \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\) (570) turns a pair of isomorphisms into an isomorphism of tensors.

Definition 609 Inverse on \(\operatorname{Pic}X\) induced by the tensor-inverse
#

Uses

  • Definition 607
  • Definition 483
  • Lemma 486
  • Lemma 420
  • Definition 607
  • Definition 483
  • Lemma 486
  • Lemma 420

Lean declarations

  • AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.picInv

The inverse class \([M] \mapsto [N]\) on \(\operatorname{Pic}X\), where \(N\) is a chosen tensor-inverse of \(M\).

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean by \(\mathtt{Quotient.lift}\): a chosen inverse witness (\(\mathtt{Classical.choose}\) of the invertibility) is itself invertible, with the braiding (420) supplying the membership isomorphism; the choice is independent of the representative because any two tensor-inverses of \(M\) are isomorphic (486).
